content: General-purpose computer agents can be rearchitected to use 2025's state-of-the-art ICM curiosity mechanism as a dynamic memory pruning gate, where only high-curiosity unexpected observations are retained in long-term active memory rather than pruning based on recency or usage frequency. This replaces traditional static memory management with an intrinsic motivation-driven system that prioritizes novel, high-learning-value content over commonly accessed old information.
